description: Corrected gold standard test abstracts for LLM extraction validation

schema_notes.field_completeness: All fields from the schema are included for every entry. Fields not applicable or not mentioned use 'not applicable' or 'not mentioned' respectively to enable consistent scoring.
schema_notes.inference_policy: Values should be extractable from the abstract text. Inferred values (not explicitly stated) are flagged with a trailing comment in the corrections field.

abstracts.title: Integrating mobility, travel survey, and malaria case data to understand drivers of malaria importation to Zanzibar, 2022-2023
corrections: Removed 'Plasmodium falciparum' from pathogen_name: species never stated in abstract, only 'malaria'
Flagged pathogen_type as inferred: abstract does not explicitly say 'parasite'
Added 'demographic data' to data_used per bronze source (gender, occupation analyzed)
Added 'health facility' to population_setting_type (surveys at 94 health facilities)
Added data_source field with Meta products explicitly named in abstract
Changed code_available from 'unspecified' to 'no' per bronze source
Removed 'correlation' from calibration_techniques: the abstract describes a descriptive correlation finding, not a model calibration technique
Added calibration_mentioned: no
Added model_type: not mentioned (no formal model is described)
Added model_determinism: not mentioned

abstracts.title: Impact of School-Based Infection Prevention Strategies on Household COVID-19 and Respiratory Disease Outcomes: a Cross-Sectional Study

intervention_types: regular testing of teachers and staff

corrections: Removed 'Omicron' from pathogen_name: Omicron is a variant of SARS-CoV-2, not a separate pathogen; retained SARS-CoV-2
Expanded 'RSV' to 'respiratory syncytial virus' for consistency (both acceptable)
Added 'household' to population_setting_type: outcomes measured at household level
Added 'parents of school-aged children' to primary_population: survey respondents were parents
Expanded key_outcome_measures to reflect the 5 outcomes described
Added 'extracurriculars policy' to intervention_types (discussed as a prevention strategy in abstract)
Removed 'incidence' from key_outcome_measures: abstract describes odds of outcomes, not incidence rates per se

abstracts.title: Calibration of transmission-dynamic infectious disease models: A scoping review and reporting framework
corrections: Changed study_goal_category from ['methodological', 'intervention evaluation'] to ['methodological']: the review itself is methodological; it reviews models that evaluate interventions but the review's goal is methodological

Abstract appears abbreviated or fabricated — substantially shorter and less detailed than a real published abstract. This weakens its use as a gold standard entry. Consider replacing with authentic abstract text.
Added explicit 'not specified' values for fields not covered in the abbreviated abstract
Added data_used: 'published literature' since this is a scoping review

abstracts.title: Evidence of transmission and dissemination of diverse bla(NDM-5)-producing Escherichia coli clones between refugee and host communities and their environment: a multicenter cross-sectional study
corrections: Added pathogen_type: ['bacterium'] — was missing entirely from original gold standard
Added historical_vs_hypothetical: ['historical'] — was missing from original
Added disease_name with inferred flag: abstract describes AMR spread rather than a named disease
Added 'refugees', 'hospitalized patients', 'host community members' to primary_population (explicitly described in abstract)
Added 'pathogen characterization' to study_goal_category per bronze source ('characterization of bacterial diversity')
Changed study_goal_category to include both transmission dynamics and characterization
Added model_type and model_determinism as 'not applicable' — this is a genomic epidemiology study, not a mathematical model
Note: bronze source lists 'proteomics' in data_used (referring to MALDI-TOF MS); kept as technique rather than data type

Moved MALDI-TOF, WGS, SNP analysis to calibration_techniques — these are analytical techniques, not calibration per se, but retained for consistency with bronze source labeling. Consider renaming field to 'analytical_techniques'.

abstracts.title: Heterogeneity in seasonal influenza vaccination opportunity and disease risk during pregnancy and in infants <6 months
corrections: Added disease_name: ['influenza'] — was missing from original
Added historical_vs_hypothetical: ['historical'] — was missing from original (bronze source also left blank but data is clearly historical)
Added population_setting_type: ['community'] — was missing from original
Added model_type and model_determinism as 'not mentioned' — this is descriptive epidemiological analysis
Added study_dates_excluded field to capture the excluded seasons noted in abstract
Added data_source as 'not specified' — abstract does not name specific databases

abstracts.title: A deep learning approach for enhancing pandemic prediction: A retrospective evaluation of transformer neural networks and multi-source data fusion for infectious disease forecasting
corrections: Flagged pathogen_name 'SARS-CoV-2' as inferred: abstract only says 'Covid-19' and 'Omicron variant', never explicitly names SARS-CoV-2
Added pathogen_type: ['virus (inferred)'] — was missing from original
Added host_species: ['human (inferred)'] — was missing from original
Flagged geographic_units 'United States' as inferred: abstract says 'county-level' but never explicitly names the US
Removed 'Omicron' from pathogen_name: it is a variant designation, not a pathogen name
Changed 'machine learning' to 'transformer neural network' in model_type: more specific and directly stated in abstract
Changed intervention_present from 'unspecified' to 'not mentioned' for clarity
Added 'methodological' to study_goal_category: abstract explicitly frames this as a methodological framework

abstracts.title: Generative diffusion model surrogates for mechanistic agent-based biological models
corrections: This paper is about in vitro vasculogenesis (biological tissue modeling), NOT infectious disease. Including it tests whether an extractor correctly identifies non-ID content. Bronze source notes 'not really applicable'.
Changed all epidemiological fields to 'not applicable' rather than omitting them, for consistent scoring
Removed 'in vitro vasculogenesis' from key_outcome_measures: that is the application domain, not an outcome measure. Replaced with computational/methodological outcomes actually measured.
Removed 'CPM' and 'DDPM' acronyms from calibration_techniques in favor of spelled-out names for clarity

abstracts.title: Collateral effects of COVID-19 pandemic control on the US infectious disease landscape
corrections: Changed pathogen_type from ['virus'] to ['multiple (virus, bacterium)']: STIs include bacterial pathogens (syphilis, gonorrhea, chlamydia). Original was incorrect.
Removed 'common pathogens' and 'STIs' from pathogen_name: these are categories, not pathogen names. Retained SARS-CoV-2 as the named pathogen.
Removed 'BSTS' and 'statistical' from model_type: BSTS is already listed, 'statistical' is too generic
Changed intervention_present to 'yes' with qualifier: the study explicitly examines collateral effects of pandemic control measures, which are interventions
Removed 'disease system properties' from data_used: serial interval, R0, susceptible recruitment are epidemiological parameters used in analysis, not input data
Removed 'immune deficits' and 'indirect effects' from key_outcome_measures: these are study topics, not measured outcomes. Replaced with measurable outcomes from abstract.
